texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Appendix F - Implementing intradialytic exercise
texts.text: Appendix F - Implementing intradialytic exercise

texts.label: text
texts.orig: We suggest the following guidance for implementation of intradialytic exercise:
texts.text: We suggest the following guidance for implementation of intradialytic exercise:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a. Exercise should be supervised for greatest compliance and efficacy by an appropriately trained individual (e.g. physiotherapist, sport scientist, cardiac rehabilitation specialist or an assistant physiotherapist/dietitian/nurse with additional training from one of the former groups).
texts.text: Exercise should be supervised for greatest compliance and efficacy by an appropriately trained individual (e.g. physiotherapist, sport scientist, cardiac rehabilitation specialist or an assistant physiotherapist/dietitian/nurse with additional training from one of the former groups).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b. Exercise should be completed between 30 min and two hours of the dialysis procedure.
texts.text: Exercise should be completed between 30 min and two hours of the dialysis procedure.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c. Exercise should be completed during all dialysis sessions (unless contraindicated).
texts.text: Exercise should be completed during all dialysis sessions (unless contraindicated).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: d. Exercise  should  include  both  aerobic  (e.g.  intradialytic  cycling)  and  resistance  (e.g.  TheraBands  and/or lifting of ankle weights) components of lower or upper body, dependent on access site.
texts.text: Exercise  should  include  both  aerobic  (e.g.  intradialytic  cycling)  and  resistance  (e.g.  TheraBands  and/or lifting of ankle weights) components of lower or upper body, dependent on access site.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: e. Exercise  should  be  preceded  by  warm  up  activities  (e.g.  exercising  for  a  minimum  of  five  minutes, gradually increasing intensity until one half of prescribed training intensity is obtained).
texts.text: Exercise  should  be  preceded  by  warm  up  activities  (e.g.  exercising  for  a  minimum  of  five  minutes, gradually increasing intensity until one half of prescribed training intensity is obtained).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: f. Exercise should be completed for at least 30 minutes per hemodialysis session.
texts.text: Exercise should be completed for at least 30 minutes per hemodialysis session.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: g. Exercise should be completed at moderate to vigorous intensity, ranging from 40-75% of VO2 reserve or heart  rate  reserve  (if  a  graded  exercise  test  is  completed). When  a  graded  exercise  test  cannot  be completed, and for muscle conditioning exercises, a rating of perceived exertion on the Borg CR100 scale of 'moderate' (20) to 'strong heavy (50)' or on the Borg RPE scale of 12 to 15 can be used with caution as a method to prescribe exercise intensity.
texts.text: Exercise should be completed at moderate to vigorous intensity, ranging from 40-75% of VO2 reserve or heart  rate  reserve  (if  a  graded  exercise  test  is  completed). When  a  graded  exercise  test  cannot  be completed, and for muscle conditioning exercises, a rating of perceived exertion on the Borg CR100 scale of 'moderate' (20) to 'strong heavy (50)' or on the Borg RPE scale of 12 to 15 can be used with caution as a method to prescribe exercise intensity.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: h. Volume  of  exercise  should  be  progressed  gradually  by  adjusting  duration,  frequency,  and/or  intensity until the desired exercise goal (maintenance) is attained.
texts.text: Volume  of  exercise  should  be  progressed  gradually  by  adjusting  duration,  frequency,  and/or  intensity until the desired exercise goal (maintenance) is attained.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: i. Exercise  should  be  followed  by  cool  down  activities  (e.g.  exercising  for  a  minimum  of  five  minutes, starting  at  one  half  of  prescribed  training  intensity  and  gradually  decreasing  intensity  until  exercise  is stopped).
texts.text: Exercise  should  be  followed  by  cool  down  activities  (e.g.  exercising  for  a  minimum  of  five  minutes, starting  at  one  half  of  prescribed  training  intensity  and  gradually  decreasing  intensity  until  exercise  is stopped).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: j. Once  patients  are  established  exercising  during  dialysis,  they  should  be  encouraged  to  complete additional exercise on non-dialysis days.
texts.text: Once  patients  are  established  exercising  during  dialysis,  they  should  be  encouraged  to  complete additional exercise on non-dialysis days.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: k. To  enhance  adoption  and  adherence  in  novice  exercisers,  moderate-intensity  activity  should  be prescribed.
texts.text: To  enhance  adoption  and  adherence  in  novice  exercisers,  moderate-intensity  activity  should  be prescribed.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: l. To  maintain  exercise behaviour,  behavioural  strategies  such  as  social support,  goal setting  and motivational interviewing should be implemented.
texts.text: To  maintain  exercise behaviour,  behavioural  strategies  such  as  social support,  goal setting  and motivational interviewing should be implemented.

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: Renal Association Clinical Practice Guideline Haemodialysis- July 2019
texts.text: Renal Association Clinical Practice Guideline Haemodialysis- July 2019

texts.label: text
texts.orig: We suggest the following patient exclusion criteria/contraindications to exercise during haemodialysis:
texts.text: We suggest the following patient exclusion criteria/contraindications to exercise during haemodialysis:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a. Less than three months after initiation of haemodialysis.
texts.text: Less than three months after initiation of haemodialysis.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b. Any  uncontrolled  medical  condition  including  (but  not  limited  to)  infection  or  fever;  recent  (within  8 weeks) myocardial infarction or undiagnosed chest pain.
texts.text: Any  uncontrolled  medical  condition  including  (but  not  limited  to)  infection  or  fever;  recent  (within  8 weeks) myocardial infarction or undiagnosed chest pain.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c. Patient  in  class  D  (unstable  condition)  as  per  American  Heart  Association/American  College  of  Sports Medicine  Joint  Position  Statement:  1)  unstable  ischemia;  2)  heart  failure  that  is  not  compenstated;3) uncontrolled arrhythmias; 4) severe and symptomatic aortic stenosis; 5) hypertrophic cardiomyopathy or cardiomyopathy from recent myocarditis; 6) severe pulmonary hypertension; or 7) other conditions that could  be  aggravated  by  exercise  (for  example,  resting  systolic  blood  pressure  >200  mm  Hg  or  resting diastolic blood pressure >110 mm Hg; active or suspected myocarditis or pericarditis; suspected or known dissecting aneurysm; thrombophlebitis and recent systemic or pulmonary embolus).
texts.text: Patient  in  class  D  (unstable  condition)  as  per  American  Heart  Association/American  College  of  Sports Medicine  Joint  Position  Statement:  1)  unstable  ischemia;  2)  heart  failure  that  is  not  compenstated;3) uncontrolled arrhythmias; 4) severe and symptomatic aortic stenosis; 5) hypertrophic cardiomyopathy or cardiomyopathy from recent myocarditis; 6) severe pulmonary hypertension; or 7) other conditions that could  be  aggravated  by  exercise  (for  example,  resting  systolic  blood  pressure  >200  mm  Hg  or  resting diastolic blood pressure >110 mm Hg; active or suspected myocarditis or pericarditis; suspected or known dissecting aneurysm; thrombophlebitis and recent systemic or pulmonary embolus).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: d. Symptomatic hyper- or hypotension.
texts.text: Symptomatic hyper- or hypotension.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: e. Signs and symptoms of deep vein thrombosis.
texts.text: Signs and symptoms of deep vein thrombosis.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: f. Excessive  inter-dialytic  weight  gain  that  severely  impacts  upon  indices  of  fluid  retention,  e.g.  blood pressure greater than 160/100; heart rate above 100 bpm; breathlessness at rest; or signs of substantial peripheral oedema.
texts.text: Excessive  inter-dialytic  weight  gain  that  severely  impacts  upon  indices  of  fluid  retention,  e.g.  blood pressure greater than 160/100; heart rate above 100 bpm; breathlessness at rest; or signs of substantial peripheral oedema.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: g. If diabetic, blood glucose above 16.7 mmol/L (300 mg/dL) AND patient is in ketosis (fruity breath, rapid breathing  or  shortness  of  breath,  excessive  thirst,  frequent  urination,  stomach  pain,  nausea,  vomiting, fatigue or confusion), is dehydrated, or is feeling unwell.
texts.text: If diabetic, blood glucose above 16.7 mmol/L (300 mg/dL) AND patient is in ketosis (fruity breath, rapid breathing  or  shortness  of  breath,  excessive  thirst,  frequent  urination,  stomach  pain,  nausea,  vomiting, fatigue or confusion), is dehydrated, or is feeling unwell.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: h. In individuals taking insulin and/or insulin secretagogues, if hypoglycaemia below 5.5 mmol/L (100 mg/dL) is observed, and glucose containing dialysate fails to elevate blood glucose sufficiently, advise patient to eat  or  drink  approximately  15g  carbohydrate  and  re-assess  blood  glucose  concentration  after  20  min. Repeat until blood glucose exceeds 5.5 mmol/L.
texts.text: In individuals taking insulin and/or insulin secretagogues, if hypoglycaemia below 5.5 mmol/L (100 mg/dL) is observed, and glucose containing dialysate fails to elevate blood glucose sufficiently, advise patient to eat  or  drink  approximately  15g  carbohydrate  and  re-assess  blood  glucose  concentration  after  20  min. Repeat until blood glucose exceeds 5.5 mmol/L.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: We suggest the following safety monitoring:
texts.text: We suggest the following safety monitoring:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a. Prior to exercise, ask patient how they feel, record resting blood pressure and heart rate, and if diabetic, record blood glucose concentration.
texts.text: Prior to exercise, ask patient how they feel, record resting blood pressure and heart rate, and if diabetic, record blood glucose concentration.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b. During  exercise,  monitor  signs  and  ask  patient  to  report  symptoms  of  pain,  excessive  fatigue,  altered consciousness, overheating, cyanosis, anxiety, severe breathlessness, chest pain, dizziness/lightheadedness.
texts.text: During  exercise,  monitor  signs  and  ask  patient  to  report  symptoms  of  pain,  excessive  fatigue,  altered consciousness, overheating, cyanosis, anxiety, severe breathlessness, chest pain, dizziness/lightheadedness.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c. If hypertensive, regularly check blood pressure during exercise. If values exceed 220/105 mm Hg, reduce exercise intensity or cease exercising until blood pressure reduces.
texts.text: If hypertensive, regularly check blood pressure during exercise. If values exceed 220/105 mm Hg, reduce exercise intensity or cease exercising until blood pressure reduces.

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: Renal Association Clinical Practice Guideline Haemodialysis- July 2019
texts.text: Renal Association Clinical Practice Guideline Haemodialysis- July 2019

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: d. Use a rating of perceived exertion scale (Borg CR100 or Borg RPE scales) and ensure exercise intensity does not provoke responses greater than 50/strong heavy on the Borg CR100 or 15/hard (heavy) on the Borg RPE scale
texts.text: Use a rating of perceived exertion scale (Borg CR100 or Borg RPE scales) and ensure exercise intensity does not provoke responses greater than 50/strong heavy on the Borg CR100 or 15/hard (heavy) on the Borg RPE scale

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: e. Post  exercise,  monitor  blood  pressure  and  heart  rate  until  resting  values  are  approximately  obtained, observe patient for at least 20 min, and be aware of possibility of hypotension during remainder of the dialysis session.
texts.text: Post  exercise,  monitor  blood  pressure  and  heart  rate  until  resting  values  are  approximately  obtained, observe patient for at least 20 min, and be aware of possibility of hypotension during remainder of the dialysis session.
